title: biocEDAM: ontology for a genomic data science ecosystem
text: biocViews package collects organizes terms tagging resources Bioconductor ecosystem genomic data science. November 2023 497 terms defining classes resources project. Example terms “Organism”, “BiologicalQuestion”, “Sequencing”, “MicroarrayData”. Contributors core members assign tags vocabulary software packages, data resources, workflows managed distributed project. BiocPkgTools package managing functions interrogate aspects ecosystem. obtain table software packages examine views:
code: library(BiocPkgTools) bl = biocPkgList(repo="BioCsoft") library(dplyr) s1 = bl |> select(Package, biocViews) s1$tags = sapply(s1$biocViews, paste, collapse=":") s1 = s1 |> select(Package, tags) set.seed(1234) s1[sample(seq_len(nrow(s1)), 10),] ## # A tibble: 10 × 2 ##    Package        tags                                                           ##    <chr>          <chr>                                                          ##  1 HiLDA          Software:BiologicalQuestion:Technology:StatisticalMethod:Soma… ##  2 doseR          Software:Infrastructure:Technology:AssayDomain:ResearchField:… ##  3 graph          Software:StatisticalMethod:GraphAndNetwork                     ##  4 cn.farms       Software:Technology:AssayDomain:Microarray:CopyNumberVariation ##  5 sSeq           Software:ResearchField:Immunology:Technology:Sequencing:Immun… ##  6 plotgardener   Software:WorkflowStep:BiologicalQuestion:ResearchField:Techno… ##  7 yamss          Software:Technology:ResearchField:BiologicalQuestion:MassSpec… ##  8 iSEEpathways   Software:BiologicalQuestion:AssayDomain:Infrastructure:Workfl… ##  9 CAFE           Software:AssayDomain:Technology:Microarray:BiologicalQuestion… ## 10 tidyexposomics Software:ResearchField:AssayDomain:BiologicalQuestion:Workflo…

title: biocEDAM: ontology for a genomic data science ecosystem
text: edamontology.org, EDAM described “comprehensive ontology well-established, familiar concepts prevalent within bioscientific data analysis data management (including computational biology, bioinformatics, bioimage informatics). EDAM includes topics, operations, types data data identifiers, data formats, relevant data analysis data management life sciences.” devel version ontoProc, ingest sample EDAM ontology: main organizing categories EDAM “data”, “format”, “operation” “topic”.
code: library(ontoProc) ## Warning: multiple methods tables found for 'scale' ## Warning: replacing previous import 'BiocGenerics::scale' by ## 'DelayedArray::scale' when loading 'SummarizedExperiment' epath = owl2cache(url="https://edamontology.org/EDAM_1.25.owl") edam = setup_entities2(epath) set.seed(1234) sam = sample(edam$name, 15) edam = names(sam) phr = as.character(sam) DT::datatable(data.frame(edam,phr))

what: A preliminary comparison of the vocabularies
title: biocEDAM: ontology for a genomic data science ecosystem
text: Pypi package text2term used measure similarity terms available EDAM terms biocViews. biocEDAM package includes table results, filter scores exceeding 0.8. Similar programming can used examine biocViews terms low maximum scores matched EDAM. indicate vocabulary gaps filled EDAM, suggest alternative tagging methodology. example, biocViews includes “ExomeSeq”. achieved scores .70, .50, .39 EDAM terms Exome sequencing, Exome assembly, “geneseq” respectively. 9 software packages Bioconductor 3.18 annotated ExomeSeq. Dissection contents additional views terms helpful understanding process needed bridge EDAM Bioconductor improved discoverability packages data.
code: library(biocEDAM) data(allmap) ndf = allmap |> filter(`Mapping Score`>.8) |> select(`Source Term`,                `Mapped Term Label`, `Mapping Score`) |> as.data.frame() library(DT) datatable(ndf)

Curation of Bioconductor package metadata, targeting EDAM ontology and ELIXIR bio.tools metadata schemas
text: vignette derived almost entirely collaborative code supplied Anh Nguyet Vu Sage Bionetworks. purpose illustrate usage OpenAPI transformation provide systematic organization tagging content available Bioconductor packages. Code vignette requires OPENAI_API_KEY defined.

title: Curation of Bioconductor package metadata, targeting EDAM ontology and ELIXIR bio.tools metadata schemas
text: start transformation vignette content, may HTML PDF, based structured data extraction code examples given vignette ellmer package CRAN. prompt GPT-4o produce concise objective summary 450 words, placed focus component returned data. use schema-driven inference produce associated EDAM tags; see code inst/curbioc package source.
code: if (nchar(Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"))>0) { library(biocEDAM) content = vig2data("https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/vignettes/tximeta/inst/doc/tximeta.html") str(content) nchar(content$focus) } if (nchar(Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"))>0) { substr(content$focus,1,250) pks = c("requests", "openai", "jsonschema", "tiktoken", "pandas") for (i in pks) reticulate::py_require(i) for (i in pks) reticulate::import(i) ans = edamize(content$focus) DT::datatable(mkdf(ans)) }

title: Curation of Bioconductor package metadata, targeting EDAM ontology and ELIXIR bio.tools metadata schemas
code: if (nchar(Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"))>0) { mm = vig2data("https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/vignettes/MSnbase/inst/doc/v05-MSnbase-development.html") uu = edamize(mm$focus) if (is.null(uu)) uu = edamize(mm$focus)  # second try DT::datatable(mkdf(uu)) }

title: Assign EDAM ontology terms to text using a live SemanticSQL database and an LLM — edamize
code: edamize(   content_for_edam,   provider = "anthropic",   model = "claude-sonnet-4-5",   nterms = 20L,   prescrub = TRUE,   prompt = read_prompt("edamize.txt"),   retrieve_k = 75L,   sim_threshold = 0.3,   embed_model = "text-embedding-3-small",   ... )

title: Assign EDAM ontology terms to text using a live SemanticSQL database and an LLM — edamize
text: content_for_edam character(1) text describing bioinformatics resource provider character(1) LLM provider; see llm_env_var. Defaults "anthropic". model character(1) model identifier selected provider. Defaults "claude-sonnet-4-5". nterms integer(1) approximate number EDAM terms select. Defaults 20. prescrub logical(1) TRUE apply cleantxt processing. Defaults TRUE. prompt character(1) instruction text sent LLM EDAM vocabulary tables content.  string must contain one %d placeholder replaced nterms.  Defaults contents inst/prompts/edamize.txt; supply string customise curation behaviour without editing package files. retrieve_k integer(1) NULL.  NULL, use embedding-based retrieval (via retrieve_edam_candidates) pre-filter EDAM vocabulary top retrieve_k candidates per type LLM selection.  Requires OPENAI_API_KEY pre-computed embedding artifact (see get_edam_embeddings). Set NULL pass full vocabulary directly LLM. Defaults 75L. sim_threshold numeric(1) minimum cosine similarity candidate term passed LLM.  Terms threshold dropped LLM selection step, reducing irrelevant tags.  used retrieve_k NULL.  Defaults 0.3. embed_model character(1) OpenAI embedding model used retrieval; must match model used build artifact. Defaults "text-embedding-3-small". ... passed llm_chat

title: Assign EDAM ontology terms to text using a live SemanticSQL database and an LLM — edamize
text: data.frame columns uri (full EDAM URI) tm (term label), restricted confirmed vocabulary entries deduplicated.  Compatible mkdf, toline, edam_graph.

title: Assign EDAM ontology terms to text using a live SemanticSQL database and an LLM — edamize
text: function replaces former Python/curbioc.py implementation. connects current EDAM release via ontoProc2::semsql_connect() selects terms using chat_structured() via ellmer, JSON schema validation loop needed hallucinated term labels eliminated post-filtering actual vocabulary.

title: Assign EDAM ontology terms to text using a live SemanticSQL database and an LLM — edamize
code: # Input validation fires without any API key tryCatch(edamize(list(a=1)), error = function(e) conditionMessage(e)) #> [1] "content_for_edam must be a single character string; did you mean to pass e.g. tst$focused?"  if (interactive() &&     nchar(Sys.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")) > 0 &&     nchar(Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")) > 0) {   content <- readRDS(system.file("rds/tximetaFocused.rds", package="biocEDAM"))   lk <- edamize(content$focused)   # retrieve_k=75 uses bundled embeddings   print(lk)   # Skip embedding retrieval and pass full vocabulary to the LLM:   lk2 <- edamize(content$focused, retrieve_k = NULL)   print(lk2) }

title: simple tagger based on text excerpt and available context from data-frames with edam vocabularies — flat_tagger
code: if (interactive()) { txt = "The Voyager package is an R/Bioconductor software designed for exploratory spatial  data analysis (ESDA) of spatial single-cell omics datasets. It operates on the  SpatialFeatureExperiment (SFE) S4 class, allowing users to perform a wide range of spatial  statistical analyses directly within a biological context. Univariate global spatial statistics  supported include Moran's I for measuring spatial autocorrelation, permutation testing  for assessing significance, and correlograms for examining spatial correlation structure.  Bivariate spatial statistics implemented in Voyager comprise Lees L statistic  and cross variograms for evaluating spatial associations between two  variables. In addition, Voyager provides tools for multivariate analysis using methods  such as MULTISPATI PCA, which integrates spatial structure into principal component  analysis, and Anselins recent multivariate local Gearys C"  flat_tagger(txt, nterms=12, model="gpt-4o") }

title: Retrieve pre-computed EDAM term embeddings from AnnotationHub — get_edam_embeddings
text: Lookup order: EDAM_EMBEDDING_RDS set readable .rds path,         file loaded returned immediately. file bundled package         inst/demo_embedding/edam_embeddings.rds used (via         system.file). AnnotationHub queried biocEDAM embedding resource. override bundled demo file freshly generated artifact, set EDAM_EMBEDDING_RDS path ensure resource AnnotationHub (see make_edam_embeddings).

title: Retrieve pre-computed EDAM term embeddings from AnnotationHub — get_edam_embeddings
code: emb <- get_edam_embeddings() #> Loading bundled EDAM embeddings from /private/var/folders/yw/gfhgh7k565v9w83x_k764wbc0000gp/T/RtmpMjVNCm/temp_libpathad7f6dc6f6fe/biocEDAM/demo_embedding/edam_embeddings.rds cat(sprintf("%d terms | %d dimensions | model: %s\n",     length(emb$ids), ncol(emb$embeddings), emb$model)) #> 2399 terms | 1536 dimensions | model: text-embedding-3-small

code: if (interactive() && nchar(Sys.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")) > 0) {     ch <- llm_chat("anthropic", model = "claude-haiku-4-5")     ch$chat("Name one EDAM topic term.") }

Map an LLM provider name to its environment variable — llm_env_var
text: provider character(1) one "openai", "anthropic", "claude", "gemini", "google", "ollama"

title: Map an LLM provider name to its environment variable — llm_env_var
text: character(1) environment variable name, "" keyless providers

title: Map an LLM provider name to its environment variable — llm_env_var
code: llm_env_var("openai") #> [1] "OPENAI_API_KEY" llm_env_var("anthropic") #> [1] "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" llm_env_var("ollama")   # "" — ollama needs no key #> [1] ""

what: Generate and save EDAM term embeddings — make_edam_embeddings
title: Generate and save EDAM term embeddings — make_edam_embeddings
text: Connects current EDAM SemanticSQL release, fetches term labels definitions, embeds using specified provider, saves result outfile.  saved object can submitted AnnotationHub loaded directly via get_edam_embeddings.

title: Generate and save EDAM term embeddings — make_edam_embeddings
Generate and save EDAM term embeddings — make_edam_embeddings
text: outfile character(1) path output .rds file. Defaults edam_embeddings.rds tempdir(). model character(1) embedding model identifier. provider="openai" use e.g. "text-embedding-3-small"; provider="huggingface" use HuggingFace model ID "FremyCompany/BioLORD-2023-C". provider character(1) embedding provider: "openai" (default) "huggingface".  corresponding environment variable must set (see llm_env_var).

title: Generate and save EDAM term embeddings — make_edam_embeddings
text: invisibly, embedding list (structure AnnotationHub resource returned get_edam_embeddings).

title: Generate and save EDAM term embeddings — make_edam_embeddings
code: if (interactive() && nchar(Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")) > 0) {     out <- file.path(tempdir(), "edam_test.rds")     emb <- make_edam_embeddings(outfile = out)     cat("Terms embedded:", length(emb$ids), "\n")     unlink(out) }

what: Map biological or medical concepts to ontology terms via OLS4 — map_concepts
title: Map biological or medical concepts to ontology terms via OLS4 — map_concepts
text: Sends query ellmer chat EBI OLS4 search tools registered, asking LLM identify concepts retrieve best matching ontology terms.  Returns typed data frame conforming TermMappingTable.

title: Map biological or medical concepts to ontology terms via OLS4 — map_concepts
code: map_concepts(   query,   provider = "anthropic",   model = "claude-sonnet-4-5",   temperature = 0,   prompt = read_prompt("map_concepts.txt"),   label_match = FALSE,   chat = ols4_chat(provider = provider, model = model, temperature = temperature) )

title: Map biological or medical concepts to ontology terms via OLS4 — map_concepts
text: query character(1) free-text input containing one biological medical concepts. provider character(1) LLM provider; see llm_env_var. Defaults "anthropic". model character(1) model identifier chosen provider. Defaults "claude-sonnet-4-5". temperature numeric(1) sampling temperature; defaults 0 deterministic output.  Ignored chat supplied directly. prompt character(1) instruction text sent LLM input text.  Defaults contents inst/prompts/map_concepts.txt; use read_prompt("map_concepts.txt") inspect customise. label_match logical(1) TRUE, adds llm_label label_match columns via ols4_enrich, flagging rows LLM label OLS4 canonical label share content words (sign hallucinated IRI).  Defaults FALSE. chat ellmer Chat object OLS4 tools already registered. supplied, provider, model, temperature ignored.  Build ols4_chat reuse across calls avoid restarting bridge process time.

title: Map biological or medical concepts to ontology terms via OLS4 — map_concepts
text: data.frame columns input_text, term_label, term_iri, obo_id, ontology, rationale, validated, definition, one row per concept-term pair (plus llm_label label_match label_match=TRUE). Filter validated == TRUE retain OLS4-confirmed terms.

title: Map biological or medical concepts to ontology terms via OLS4 — map_concepts
text: row validated EBI OLS4 REST API via ols4_enrich: rows whose term_iri found OLS4 dropped warning, authoritative definition column added alongside LLM-generated rationale.

title: Map biological or medical concepts to ontology terms via OLS4 — map_concepts
code: if (interactive()) {     # default provider (anthropic)     map_concepts("spatial autocorrelation in gene expression")      # switch provider     map_concepts("chromatin accessibility",                  provider = "openai", model = "gpt-4o")      # reuse a pre-built chat across multiple calls     ch <- ols4_chat()     map_concepts("atrial fibrillation", chat = ch)     map_concepts("whole genome sequencing", chat = ch) }

what: Export a map_concepts result as a JSON document — mapping_to_json
title: Export a map_concepts result as a JSON document — mapping_to_json
text: Converts data.frame returned map_concepts JSON document.  Terms grouped ontology; entry records ontology IRI, term label, OBO identifier, input concept mapped.

title: Export a map_concepts result as a JSON document — mapping_to_json
Export a map_concepts result as a JSON document — mapping_to_json
text: x data.frame returned map_concepts. validated_only logical(1) TRUE (default), rows validated == TRUE included. path character(1) NULL.  NULL, JSON written file path NULL returned invisibly.  NULL (default) JSON string returned.

title: Export a map_concepts result as a JSON document — mapping_to_json
text: JSON character string, NULL invisibly path   supplied.

title: Export a map_concepts result as a JSON document — mapping_to_json
code: df <- data.frame(   input_text = c("atrial fibrillation", "genome sequencing"),   term_label = c("Atrial Fibrillation", "Whole Genome Sequencing"),   term_iri   = c("http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/HP_0005110",                  "http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002117"),   obo_id     = c("HP:0005110", "OBI:0002117"),   ontology   = c("HP", "OBI"),   rationale  = c("matches concept", "matches concept"),   validated  = c(TRUE, TRUE),   definition = c(NA_character_, NA_character_),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) cat(mapping_to_json(df)) #> { #>   "HP": [ #>     { #>       "input_text": "atrial fibrillation", #>       "term_label": "Atrial Fibrillation", #>       "term_iri": "http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/HP_0005110", #>       "obo_id": "HP:0005110" #>     } #>   ], #>   "OBI": [ #>     { #>       "input_text": "genome sequencing", #>       "term_label": "Whole Genome Sequencing", #>       "term_iri": "http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0002117", #>       "obo_id": "OBI:0002117" #>     } #>   ] #> }

what: Create an ellmer chat pre-wired with EBI OLS4 MCP tools — ols4_chat
title: Create an ellmer chat pre-wired with EBI OLS4 MCP tools — ols4_chat
text: Calls ols4_mcp_tools retrieve 12 OLS4 search tools, creates ellmer chat object via llm_chat, registers tools chat LLM can invoke structured calls.

title: Create an ellmer chat pre-wired with EBI OLS4 MCP tools — ols4_chat
Create an ellmer chat pre-wired with EBI OLS4 MCP tools — ols4_chat
text: provider character(1) LLM provider; see llm_env_var. Defaults "anthropic". model character(1) model identifier. Defaults "claude-sonnet-4-5". temperature numeric(1) sampling temperature passed LLM. Defaults 0 deterministic, reproducible output. url character(1) OLS4 MCP endpoint URL passed ols4_mcp_tools. ... additional arguments passed llm_chat.

title: Create an ellmer chat pre-wired with EBI OLS4 MCP tools — ols4_chat
text: ellmer Chat object OLS4 tools registered.

title: Create an ellmer chat pre-wired with EBI OLS4 MCP tools — ols4_chat
text: Starting mcp-remote bridge takes seconds. Reuse returned object across multiple calls rather creating new one time.

title: Create an ellmer chat pre-wired with EBI OLS4 MCP tools — ols4_chat
code: if (interactive()) {     ch <- ols4_chat()     map_concepts("chromatin accessibility and histone modification", chat = ch) }

what: Validate and enrich a TermMappingTable via the OLS4 REST API — ols4_enrich
title: Validate and enrich a TermMappingTable via the OLS4 REST API — ols4_enrich
text: row result, queries EBI OLS4 REST API term_iri confirm term exists retrieve authoritative definition.  rows retained; two columns added: validated logical — TRUE IRI found OLS4,     FALSE (possible hallucination deprecated term) definition character — OLS4-sourced definition validated     terms; NA unvalidated rows

title: Validate and enrich a TermMappingTable via the OLS4 REST API — ols4_enrich
Validate and enrich a TermMappingTable via the OLS4 REST API — ols4_enrich
text: result data.frame returned map_concepts. label_match logical(1) TRUE, add llm_label label_match columns (content-word overlap check LLM label OLS4 canonical label).  Defaults FALSE.

title: Validate and enrich a TermMappingTable via the OLS4 REST API — ols4_enrich
text: result validated definition columns added, optionally llm_label label_match.

title: Validate and enrich a TermMappingTable via the OLS4 REST API — ols4_enrich
text: Three columns added updated: llm_label original label produced LLM, preserved     replaced OLS4 canonical label label_match logical — TRUE LLM label OLS4     canonical label share least one content word (basic semantic     consistency check); FALSE flags cases LLM supplied     real unrelated IRI (e.g. "variant calling" → "Cystatin-SN");     NA unvalidated rows definition OLS4-sourced definition; NA unvalidated rows Rows validated = TRUE label_match = FALSE suspicious: IRI exists OLS4 likely correspond extracted concept.

title: Validate and enrich a TermMappingTable via the OLS4 REST API — ols4_enrich
code: if (interactive()) {     ch  <- ols4_chat()     raw <- map_concepts("atrial fibrillation and genome sequencing", chat = ch)     enr <- ols4_enrich(raw)     enr[enr$validated, ]   # confirmed terms only }

List tools available from the EBI OLS4 MCP service — ols4_mcp_tools

title: List tools available from the EBI OLS4 MCP service — ols4_mcp_tools

title: List tools available from the EBI OLS4 MCP service — ols4_mcp_tools

what: Read a prompt template from the biocEDAM prompt library — read_prompt
title: Read a prompt template from the biocEDAM prompt library — read_prompt
text: Loads plain-text prompt file inst/prompts/ name. Use inspect default prompt function, load starting point modifying passing prompt= parameter edamize, map_concepts, flat_tagger.

title: Read a prompt template from the biocEDAM prompt library — read_prompt
Read a prompt template from the biocEDAM prompt library — read_prompt
text: name character(1) filename within inst/prompts/, e.g. "edamize.txt", "map_concepts.txt", "flat_tagger.txt".

title: Read a prompt template from the biocEDAM prompt library — read_prompt
text: character(1) prompt text trailing whitespace stripped.

title: Read a prompt template from the biocEDAM prompt library — read_prompt
code: cat(read_prompt("map_concepts.txt")) #> Identify ALL biological, medical, and technical concepts in the following input text. #> For every concept found, use the OLS search tools to find the best matching ontology term. #>  #> Use the following ontology priorities: #> - EDAM: computational methods, algorithms, bioinformatics operations, data types, file formats, and software topics #> - GO: biological processes, molecular functions, and cellular components #> - HP: human phenotypes and clinical abnormalities #> - MONDO: diseases and disorders #> - ChEBI: chemical entities and small molecules #> - EFO: experimental factors, assay types, and study designs #> - PATO: phenotypic qualities and attributes #>  #> IMPORTANT: if no ontology provides a clearly relevant term for a concept, omit that concept rather than forcing a poor mapping. #> Do not map computational or statistical concepts (e.g. autocorrelation, PCA, clustering) to disease or experimental ontologies. #> The term_iri and obo_id you report must come directly from the OLS search tool results, never constructed or guessed. #>  #> Return one row per concept-term pair.

what: Retrieve the top-k semantically closest EDAM terms per type — retrieve_edam_candidates
title: Retrieve the top-k semantically closest EDAM terms per type — retrieve_edam_candidates
text: Embeds content using OpenAI API, computes cosine similarity pre-computed EDAM embedding matrix, returns top retrieve_k candidates per EDAM type (topic, operation, data, format).

title: Retrieve the top-k semantically closest EDAM terms per type — retrieve_edam_candidates
code: retrieve_edam_candidates(   content,   edam_emb,   retrieve_k = 75L,   sim_threshold = 0.3,   embed_model = edam_emb$model )

title: Retrieve the top-k semantically closest EDAM terms per type — retrieve_edam_candidates
text: content character(1) text use query (e.g. package description). edam_emb list returned get_edam_embeddings make_edam_embeddings. retrieve_k integer(1) number candidates keep per type. sim_threshold numeric(1) minimum cosine similarity; candidates value dropped LLM selection step.  Defaults 0.3. embed_model character(1) embedding model; must match model used build edam_emb.  Defaults edam_emb$model.

title: Retrieve the top-k semantically closest EDAM terms per type — retrieve_edam_candidates
text: named list data.frames (topic, operation, data, format), columns id lbl, ordered descending cosine similarity content.

title: Retrieve the top-k semantically closest EDAM terms per type — retrieve_edam_candidates
code: # Model mismatch is caught before any API call emb <- get_edam_embeddings() #> Loading bundled EDAM embeddings from /private/var/folders/yw/gfhgh7k565v9w83x_k764wbc0000gp/T/RtmpMjVNCm/temp_libpathad7f6dc6f6fe/biocEDAM/demo_embedding/edam_embeddings.rds tryCatch(     retrieve_edam_candidates("some text", emb,                              embed_model = "text-embedding-3-large"),     error = function(e) conditionMessage(e) ) #> [1] "embed_model 'text-embedding-3-large' does not match the artifact model 'text-embedding-3-small'.\nRun make_edam_embeddings(model = 'text-embedding-3-large') to generate a matching artifact."  if (interactive() && nchar(Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")) > 0) {     candidates <- retrieve_edam_candidates(         "RNA-seq transcript quantification and metadata management",         emb, retrieve_k = 5L)     candidates$topic }

Defaults "openai". ... passed underlying chat_* function via llm_chat
